incidents.narrative:
Case Number: (334) 555-5492
Call For Service Number: E244852789

 ***BWC***    On 03/19/1999 I performed a traffic stop at the listed address and arrested the driver for DUI.    While on patrol, driving northbound on Richmond Highway, I observed a white sedan making an illegal u-turn, away from an intersection and crossing the double yellow lines. The sedan appeared to make the turn slowly and continued driving slowly despite my presence behind him. I activated my ICV and began following the vehicle due to my suspicion of DUI and I observed continuous swerving within its lane of travel, and at least two occasions that it swerved into the adjacent lane. Due to this driving behavior, and the illegal U-turn I initiated a traffic stop.     The vehicle was slow to stop but sfely pulled to the side of Richmond Highway. I approached the driver side and noticed a strong odor of alcoholic beverage coming from inside the vehicle. It was occupied by two males, and the driver appeared to only speak spanish. I utilized an interpreter to speak with the driver and he gave no explanation as to his swerving behavior. He was also confused where to find his license and slow to remove it from his wallet when I asked for it. Additionally, he had difficulty finding his registration, fumbling with the glovebox and then allowing an unopened can of Modelo to fall out of the glovebox when it opened. I asked if he had anything to drink that night and he said he hadn`t.     I asked the driver to step out of the vehicle and he complied. When the door opened I saw an open can of Modelo in the driver side door pocket, and he was unsteady on his feet as he walked to the rear of the vehicle.  He was brought to the space between my cruiser and his vehicle and agreed to do SFSTs on a voluntary basis. With the assistance of interpretation, he was given the instructions and said he understood them clearly.     On the HGN test he displayed lack of smooth pursuit in both eyes, nystagmus before 45 degrees, and a distinct and sustained nystagmus in both eyes. While being given the instructions for the 9 step walk and turn he began early despite repeated instructions to wait, was unable to properly take any of the prescribed steps and stopped walking instead of turning after the 9th step. On the one legged stand, he stumbled while being given the instructions repeatedly before discontinuing the test.     He was unable to provide a sufficient sample when offered the PBT despite three attempts. At this point due to his driving behavior, the strong odor of alcoholic beverage from his person, and his apparent inebriation during the tests and interaction he was placed under arrest for DUI. He was transported to Mount Vernon Station where a breathalyzer was administered by 220A (Villatorro). A sufficient sample was provided and measured .21 BAC. At this point, the driver, Identified as Caceres-Guzman, Douglas, was transported to the ADC without incident. Warrants were obtained for 18.2-266 (DWI: 2nd w/in 5 years, BAC >.20%); 18.2-323.1 (Open Container); and a Citation was issued for 46.2-845 (Improper U-Turn). Additionally, his license was surrendered to the court pursuant to state code.    Caceres-Guzman was held on 2500 secured bond and remanded to the custody of the FCSO without incident.    Nothing further.  Closed by arrest.

incidents.narrative:
Case Number: (659) 555-4743
Call For Service Number: E237872805

BWC Active    This report was generated using Draft One.    On May 18, 2025, at approximately 11:55 PM, I responded to the above listed address for a call regarding a possible larceny from a home. Upon arrival, I was informed by the reporting party named Torres Funes, Santos Reyna, that a friend, identified Herrera Ordonez, Ellie, had been inside their apartment and was suspected of stealing valuable items from the residence.    Reyna stated that Ellie, a 16-17-year-old girl, had been invited into the apartment willingly by her daughter, Pacheco Torres, Iris Aracelis. At some point, they lost sight of her for a few seconds and the next time they would see her again was leaving Reyna`s bedroom. Ellie had asked Iris to take her home afterwards. Later on, Reyna went to her bedroom and when she checked the room where Ellie had been, she discovered that several valuable items, including 8 gold rings and approximately $650 dollars in cash, were missing. Reyna did not witness Ellie taking the items but assumed she did because the items were missing after she left the room. She had confirmed that the last time she saw them were at around 2100hrs when she arrived home and placed them in her room.    Reyna attempted to confront Ellie, who then fled the scene on foot. Ellie was described as having black hair with orange highlights, tattoos on her neck and arm, and was last seen wearing either a black or beige shirt, black pants, and black shoes. She was also carrying a black backpack.    I inquired about Ellie`s address and contact information. Iris mentioned that they did not know her full name, parents, or exact address, but Ellie had given an address to take her home. The address being 1127 Cedar Ter, Manassas Park, VA 20111. Iris also mentioned that Ellie had been acting suspiciously, touching various items in the apartment, and had worn one of the necklaces to take pictures. Iris observed this, however she did not think much of it. Iris further explained that while driving Ellie home, she received a call from Reyna informing them that her purse, containing important documents and the stolen items, was missing. Upon hearing this, Iris turned the car around and returned to the apartment. When they arrived, Reyna opened the car door where Ellie was, and she ran away. Her direction of travel was last seen headed towards Hollywood Rd Northbound.    I asked OFC Teklu to search the area around Hollywood Road, where Ellie was last seen running. Despite the effort, we were unable to locate her. I advised the reporting party to provide any additional information they might have about Ellie`s whereabouts or contact details. We were able to find Ellie`s Dad information on I Leads. Her Dad named, Herrera Martinez, Lazaro was linked to the phone number provided by Iris to which she used to communicate with Ellie. I informed the reporting party of the next steps, including reporting and following up with any new information they might receive about Ellie. I also advised them to contact the police immediately if they found more evidence to prove that Ellie had the stolen items or obtained any new information about her location.    Before leaving their residence, a civilian waved me down to inquire about the situation. He advised that he had overheard the conversation and had found a similar bag matching the description of her stolen bag. The civilian showed a picture of the bag and Reyna confirmed it was her purse. The civilian returned it and inside it contained all her documentation including her ID and Social Security Card. Unfortunately, all other stolen items were not inside the bag. The civilian did not feel comfortable providing any more information for fearing that he would get in trouble. He advised that he had found the bag in one of the bathrooms inside his building complex down in Hollywood Rd. This aligns with Ellie having the bag due to her running away towards Hollywoo...

Case Number: (846) 555-8074
Call For Service Number: E288674928

--3797 River / 1062 WillowActive--  On 12/10/1975 at 22:05hrs, this unit was on routine patrol in the area of Saint Germain Drive and Field Encampment Road in Centreville checking Centre Ridge North Park(County Park-closed after dark). The listed Chevy pickup pulled into the park behind me before parking in a parking space. When I repositioned my cruiser(unmarked) to face the truck, I observed several of the occupants exit who appeared to be checking around the vehicle. They were likely unsuspecting me being law enforcement until I pulled up to them close enough to obtain a tag number. Once they noticed me, they quickly got back in the truck and turned right onto Saint Germain Drive.  I ran the VA temporary tag "A33547" which returned to the pickup with the registered owner(Mateo Ramirez Velasco) showing wanted out of our jurisdiction for a misdemeanor FTA (original charge DWI). I followed the truck onto Saint Germain Drive where I observed it park in the parallel parking area. I initiated a traffic stop on the vehicle before the driver immediately got out to speak with me. The driver spoke little English, but thought I was stopping him regarding where he parked. I asked for him to provide me with his drivers license and he stated he left it at home. When asked where the owner of the vehicle was, he pointed at the two passengers in the car then stated he was at home pointing towards the neighborhood. I then asked him to provide me with his name and DOB. He stated his name was "Henrique Ramirez Lopez" with a 05/09/1991 DOB. I advised for him to have a seat back in the truck at which point he complied. I returned to my cruiser to run the provided information and obtain a photo of the registered owner. At that point I requested a backup officer who speaks Spanish to assist.  As I was running information to confirm, I observed the driver abruptly exit the driver seat who then fled on foot Northbound on Saint Germain Drive towards Golden Oak Road along the sidewalk. I then initiated a foot pursuit after the driver while it appeared the two passengers remained in the truck. I voiced the foot pursuit to dispatch along with my charges and yelled out commands to the subject for him to stop. I chased the subject on foot for approximately 400 feet before he cut right, onto a paved path along the wood line. I was able to catch up to the subject on the path and conducted a take down by applying my upper body momentum while wrapping my arms around the subject. My momentum and force of weight applied brought the subject to the ground. I used my weight on top of him to maintain control until I was able to place him in handcuffs. No strikes or other uses of force were applied.  I was uninjured and the subject, later identified as Mr. Mateo Ramirez Velasco, sustained minor cuts on his arms and knees from being brought down to the pavement. Rescue responded to the scene to evaluate him. He refused to be treated or be brought to the hospital.  The subject had a Guatemala ID on his person confirming him to be our wanted subject, Mateo Ramirez Velasco (01/08/1970). Mateo advised he ran because he thought I was immigration enforcement.  Mateo was read his Miranda Rights with OFC F. Negahban(120A) assisting with Spanish translation. After speaking with him, I noticed an odor of alcoholic beverage coming from his person. When asked, he stated he had two beers with his last one hour prior. SFST`s were not offered due to him being in PD custody and flight risk. He was advised of his rights under PBT and agreed to provide a voluntary breath sample. The result was a .123. Mateo was not licensed and has a DWI court case pending(reference the FTA). The warrant was confirmed by TTY and in hand at the Magistrates. He was advised he was under arrest at that time for the warrant. The vehicle was impounded by Willow Springs where open containers of beer were located.  I secured Mr. Ramirez in the ...

****BWC activated this is a summary of the BWC****  On May 18th 2025, I responded to 6076 Kestner Circle for the report of a bat that was inside of the location. I arrived on scene and spoke with the complainant Ms. Melanie Crosby and her husband. The bat was caught. A PD322 was filled out and there was a possible exposure with their cat. They own Pepper 5 year old female tabby DSH rabies expires 09/15/1997. I filled out and Animal Confinement PD302 for 45 days that ends on 11/22/1977. I went over the PD302 and later texted them a copy of the 45 day confinement information sheet. I advised them they may need to get a rabies booster for the cat. The bat was transported to the West Ox Animal Shelter. The bat was euthanized with .3 CCs of Fatalplus. The bat was placed in the rabies testing cooler. An Animal Impound and Rabies Test Form were filled out.  Case status service.

Case Number: (901) 555-2549
Call For Service Number: E262694124

 On 12/10/1975, at around 2020 hours, Officers arrived on scene to the listed address for a larceny that had just occurred. BWC was active.    Prior to Officers arrival on scene, a description of the subject was provided as wearing a black shirt and black pants with a "wooden" hat and a black trashbag. As I was arriving on scene, I saw a subject matching that description in the McDonalds parking lot. I approached the subject and detained him. Looking into the bag, Officers found multiple seafood products that appeared to have been stolen. The bag was returned to the store.    At the store, officers spoke with the complainant, Beatriz Osorio, who confirmed the description and the items that were taken. The complainant provided a receipt for the items which totaled $149.86. Osorio wanted to press charges for the larceny. No camera footage was available.    The detained subject was identified as Robert Johnson. While Officers had the subject detained, he was searched for any additional evidence of the crime with negative results.    After confirming the larceny, Johnson was issued a summons for larceny and released without incident.    Osorio stated that the seafood would have to be thrown away, since it left the store.    No other actions were taken.  Case closed arrest.

Call For Service Number: E223260990

 ***BWC***  On 12/10/1975 I responded to the listed address for a report of a disorderly subject. A fairfax connector bus driver reported that a subject (Identified as Rivas Rubi, Carlos) was on the bus and had destroyed part of it, while refusing to leave. The bus was waiting at the address without passengers when I arrived.  I spoke with the driver (TEGEGNE, Tulu) and he said that the subject had left. He told me that the subject was often on the bus and seemed drunk but did not cause problems. Today however the subject had cut the line past other passengers and stood at the door angrily. When the door opened he approached the divider and began yelling in a disorderly manner while punching and kicking the dividing door that separates the driver compartment and the passenger compartment. The driver initially called for help but hung up due to the subject`s proximity. When he had enough space to exit he left the bus and called for help while the subject finished ripping the divider off of its hinge. The subject took the door off the bus and threw it into a storm drain nearby before approaching the driver aggressively while gesturing, kicking cans at him, and yelling. The driver said he was extremely fearful of being physically assaulted but remained quiet so he did not escalate. The subject eventually walked into the middle of Richmond Highway and stood in front of a metro bus (REX line) that allowed him on and continued northbound.  The subject was described as a 5`10 white or middle eastern male, with a muscular build, short black hair and some short facial hair. He had tattoos on his hands or arms, and was wearing a green shirt, green cargo pants, and khaki combat boots. I spoke with a fairfax county supervisor on scene and I sent an email to the fairfax connector control center with a link to Evidence.com to upload the bus`s surveillance video.  Later on the evening of the 18th, I heard units dispatched to a report of a disorderly subject on a metro bus, and the subject matched the description of my subject from the Fairfax Connector incident. I responded to the scene and saw Metro Transit police already speaking with the subject from the bus. I spoke with the metro bus driver and she advised that she had picked the subject up earlier that evening at Cooper Road. Upon further questioning, the story that she gave about seeing a subject smashing up a fairfax connector bus and then stepping into the roadway before boarding the metro bus matched the narrative that I had gotten from TEGEGNE. Additionally, the physical and clothing descriptions matched what I had been given earlier in the shift. Metro transit police advised they were not taking action based on the incident aboard the metro bus, and I told RIVAS RUBI that he was under arrest for destruction of property and assault. He was also visibly intoxicated, with bloodshot glossy eyes, an overly sweaty face, and a strong odor of intoxicating beverage coming from his person. RIVAS was escorted to the front of my cruiser and searched without incident. Nothing of evidentiary value was found and he was seatbelted in the rear of Cruiser 3667 for transport to the magistrate. Enroute he repeatedly said he was fleeing from `Three black guys, you know their names already` at Lockheed boulevard and that was why he had gotten into an altercation on the metro bus. The rest of the transport was conducted without incident and warrants were obtained for 18.2-57 (Assault); 18.2-137 (Monument: Intentional Damage, Value <$1000) and 18.2-388 (Public Intoxication). He was given a 2500 dollar unsecured bond and held under he was sober.  Upon review of his profile in iLEADS I saw that RIVAS has an alert for being a member of MS13. Nothing he said, possessed, or did appeared to be related to gang activity during these encounters, but gangs will be infocopied for awareness. Sheriff`s office personnel were also alerted to his possibl...

incidents.narrative:
Case Number: (531) 555-6857
Call For Service Number: E211226016

BWC Activated    This report was generated using Draft One.    On May 18, 2025, at approximately 4:56 PM, I, Police Officer Derrick Mendez, responded to a call for service at 7-11 in 410. Upon arrival, I spoke with the store employee who reported a theft incident involving a Hispanic male and female. The male was described as wearing a white T-shirt, and the female was described as wearing a very pink shirt and short shorts.    The employee stated that the male and female entered the store at around 4:16 PM. The male did not take anything but stood around, while the female placed a beer in her bag, grabbed food, and walked out without paying. The incident was captured on the store`s surveillance camera.     The employee mentioned that the total value of the stolen items was approximately $37. I provided the employee with a business card and the case number for reference. I also collected the employee`s identification for the report.    I informed the employee to contact the police if the suspects returned to the store.     The area was checked with negative results. Photos of the subjects have were disseminated to Mason officers in an attempt to ID the subjects. At the time of this report the manager is unsure of if they want to prosecute.     Case Inactive - Pending ID of subjects     I acknowledge this report was generated using Draft One by Axon. I further acknowledge that I have reviewed the report in detail, made any necessary edits, and believe it to be an accurate representation of my recollection of the reported events. If needed, I am willing to testify to the accuracy of this report.

incidents.narrative:
Case Number: (636) 555-3338
Call For Service Number: E220131984

On May 18th, 2025, at approximately 0128 hours, I responded to the area of 9495 Willow St in reference to a suspicious person. The caller provided there was a subject in a dark colored SUV who appeared to be stopped at the light and not moving.   When I arrived on-scene I observed a green 2002 Hyundai SUV, with Virginia License Plate GAMMA10-5741, sitting stationary on Gallows Rd in the left-straight traffic-lane. The driver, a hispanic male, was slumped-over towards the driver-side front door, with the engine to the vehicle running at the time. Upon approach, I observed a small child, later discovered to be four years-old, sitting in a car seat on the passenger-side rear seat. I approached the driver who is suspect for reporting purposes, identified via name and birth date as Mr. Wilson Sanchez. Mr. Sanchez was breathing but unresponsive, as I rubbed his sternum and tapped his shoulders in an attempt to wake him up. Finally, Mr. Sanchez woke up and proceeded to place the vehicle into drive. At that moment, I grabbed Mr. Sanchez and pulled him out of the vehicle for safety concerns. Officer Vega then placed him into handcuffs while I held him.  I observed an open bottle of Jose Cuervo Tequila on the front passenger seat, in addition to several Corona cans on the floor in the front of the vehicle. While detained, Mr. Sanchez had a difficult time keeping his balance and almost fell several times. Resultantly, we were unable to administer the Standardized Field Sobriety Tests on him. Mr. Sanchez spoke primarily Spanish language so Officer Vega assisted with translation. He began crying and stated that he had a problem with alcohol. He stated he had drank all the tequila and that he was sorry. He did not specify where he was coming from.  Officer Vega contacted the mother of Mr. Sanchez`s child and had her come get their child.   I transported Mr. Sanchez to the Adult Detention Center. PFC Pak administered the breath test, which measured Mr. Sanchez`s BAC to be .16%.  I then took Mr. Sanchez before the Magistrate where I obtained charges for violation of 18.2-270: DUI While Transporting a Person 17 Years-Old or Younger, 18.2-323.1: Open Container, 46.2-300: Driving Without a License, and 46.2-888: Improper Stop on Highway.  Mr. Sanchez was given a $3500 unsecured bond.  This case is cleared via arrest.  BWC was in-use.

incidents.narrative:
Case Number: (535) 555-6607
Call For Service Number: E290164715

  BWC Activated.    On 01/18/1993 at 1527 hours, I responded to the Target located at 6600 Richmond Hwy for a Larceny. The caller, Maimona Maham (Loss Prevention) stated that two women were in the store concealing merchandise. Maimona then advised that the she had confronted the subjects, recovered the merchandise, and that the subjects had left.    Upon my arrival, I met with Maimona who stated that she was watching the two subjects, who were later identified as Skyy Diggs and Shaniya Banks, on the security cameras as they concealed clothing in bags as they walked around the store. Maimona stated she stopped Skyy and Shaniya after they had passed all points of sale and as they approached the exit doors where she was able to collect the merchandise as they exited the store. Maimona gave a description and showed a picture of the vehicle that Skyy and Shaniya had left in and stated that they drove to the shopping center across South Gate Dr. from the Target.    The merchandise consisted of $708.97 worth of clothing.    Maimona showed officers video which confirmed her story.    Images of Skyy and Shaniya were obtained and MPO. Jones and I checked the parking lot of the shopping center. MPO. Jones located the vehicle (VA Tags: 4733XT) parked in a parking spot in front of the Marshalls located at 6700 Richmond Hwy. MPO. Jones and I parked our cruiser in two different locations in area where we could see the entrance/exit of the Marshalls and the parked vehicle.    I observed Skyy exit the Marshalls and walk to the parked vehicle where she sat in the passenger seat. I then observe Shaniya exit the Marshalls and begin to walk in the direction of the parked vehicle. I pulled my cruiser up behind the vehicle as MPO. Jones was pulling his cruiser to the front of the vehicle. MPO. Jones and I both activate our emergency equipment to indicate that they were not free to leave. Skyy then exits the vehicle and begins to run away from my direction. I exit my vehicle, give her multiple commands to stop and inform her that she is under arrest (which she does not comply with), and run after her in the parking lot. Due to running on the asphalt parking lot, I did not want to injure Skyy so I attempted to grab ahold of her by her arms and shoulders to get her to stop, as I tell her to stop, and she pulls away from me and continues to run. I keep close distance with Skyy as she attempts to break away from me and, as we approach a raised mulch bed, Skyy falls over the curb and I fall on top of her. I continue to give Skyy commands to put her hands behind her back as she refuses to comply and keeps her arms tucked under her body. Skyy eventually compiles and puts her hands behind her back and I am able to apply handcuffs.    During this, MPO. Jones was able to take Shaniya into custody, conducted a search of her person and placed her into the rear of his cruiser. MPO. Jones advised that Shaniya was carrying a shirt from Marshalls that she did not pay for.    I walked Skyy back to my cruiser where I conducted a search of her person and placed her into the rear of my cruiser.    OFC. Lee went into the Marshalls where he spoke to employees who informed him that they observed Skyy and Shaniya exit the store with a gift bag full of unpaid merchandise. OFC. Lee informed myself and MPO. Jones of this larceny and located the gift bag in plain view inside of the vehicle. OFC. Lee retrieved the bag from inside of the vehicle, opened it up, and discovered the unpaid for merchandise from Marshalls. Loss Prevention Officer. Andres Oliveri reviewed the security camera footage and confirmed that Skyy and Shaniya had stolen from the store.    The stolen items consisted of $489.46 worth of clothing. The items were returned to Marshalls.    Skyy and Shaniya were both placed under arrest for two counts of Petit Larceny (18.2-96) each and Skyy for an additional charge for Obsruction (18.2-460).    I tran...

incidents.narrative:
Case Number: (562) 555-2178
Call For Service Number: E243343645

BWC & ICV in use - E212196413  On 01/23/1991 at 1:40 AM, I was on Rt. 5819 Oak Way and accelerated rapidly.  My radar displayed a speed of 105 MPH.  I caught up to the vehicle again near Leesburg Pike and made a traffic stop on the vehicle.    I spoke with the driver, Ms. Tharyn Zuniga Corado.  She stated that she was just trying to "test out" the car because she had never gone that fast.  I asked for her license and registration and if she had anything to drink.  She was unable to locate her registration.  She denied having any alcohol, but when I asked when her last drink of alcohol was, she stated it was a few hours ago.  I asked what she had to drink, and she stated she had one vodka soda.  I asked her to exit the vehicle to speak, as her passenger had attempted to answer questions for her.    I asked Tharyn if she would perform field sobriety tests, and she agreed.  Tharyn said she has no problems with her vision, no issues with balancing or walking, and she has a high school education.  English is not her first language but she stated she understood.  I performed the Horizontal Gaze Nystagmus test and observed 2 out of 6 indicators, lack of smooth pursuit in both eyes.  Tharyn had difficulty focusing on the stimulus during the check for distinct and sustained nystagmus at maximum deviation, and she explained that it was hard for her to see after I removed her glasses.  I did not observe vertical gaze nystagmus.    I explained and demonstrated the Walk and Turn test.  Tharyn started without prompting and was unable to maintain the starting position.  After starting the test, Tharyn made an improper turn, lost her balance after the turn, and stopped the test.  She took 10 steps after turning as well.  I observed 5 out of 8 indicators on this test.    I next explained and demonstrated the One Leg Stand test.  Tharyn began taking heel to toe steps again, and I explained that she was just to stand still and raise her foot up.  She attempted the test again and began walking heel to toe again.  I explained the test again, and she then was able to attempt it as instructed.  Tharyn used her arms to balance, swayed, and put her foot down several times, which are 3 out of 4 indicators on this test.  I offered her a preliminary breath test, and she agreed.  Tharyns result was 0.144.  I arrested her for DUI, searched her, and seated her in my vehicle.  I retrieved her phone, wallet, and keys at her request.  PFC Ciarrocchi remained with the vehicle and passengers until they were picked up by Henrys Towing.  A copy of the tow sheet is attached to this report.   I transported Tharyn to the ADC and administered and evidentiary breath test.  Tharyn was unable to provide a sufficient sample and complained that her mouth was very dry.  I provided her water and waited an additional 20-minute observation period.  After, Tharyn provided a sample which read 0.10.  I brought her before the magistrate, who issued warrants for DWI 1st and Reckless by Speed.  She was fingerprinted and held until sober.  7 day ALS was sent to Teletype.  Closed by arrest.

incidents.narrative:
Case Number: (491) 555-3994
Call For Service Number: E269941887

On 05/10/1998 at 1324 hours, I was dispatched to the Marshalls at 6200 Little River Tpke for a reported larceny in progress. The caller advised a 40-year-old black male in all black clothing was concealing merchandise in the men`s department. The male suspect left the store on foot towards Jollibee. Loss prevention was able to recover the products. The caller called back at 1329 hours and said that a black female wearing a blue sweater, and gray pants also stole from the store. The items were recovered, and she fled on foot towards the chipotle.    BWCA    I arrived on scene and started driving around the shopping center looking for the suspects. After searching for a while with negative results, I got out of my cruiser to check inside the nearby businesses on foot. As I approached the Chipotle, a black male in all black matching the larceny suspect exited the front of the restaurant. I saw the suspect running toward the bus stop as I was rounding the corner of the store. I followed the suspect walking quickly behind him toward the bus stop on the west bound side of Little River Tpke near Oasis Drive. I called out to the suspect telling him to stop. He looked back at me several times and continued to move away from me quickly. The suspect ran up to the bus and attempted to get on the bus but the doors were closed. The suspect then ran across Little River Tpke to the bus stop on the eastbound side of Little River Tpke. I informed dispatch that I was in a foot pursuit. He slowed down and came to a stop at the bus stop. I grabbed his arm and placed him into handcuffs that were checked for proper fit and double locked.    The suspect was identified as Michael Brown. Mr. Brown said that he was running because he was trying to catch the bus. Initially, he denied being involved in the Marshalls incident. PFC Sloane went to Marshall`s to confirm the identity of the suspect via camera footage. Marshall loss prevention worker, Thomasiere Hewlett, was able to identify Mr. Brown and said that she wanted to press charges. The other female suspect was shown to Mr. Brown and indicated that it was his girlfriend. Another family member of Mr. Brown`s arrived on scene to say the suspects name was Talonie Stewart, however, that spelling was never confirmed nor her date of birth found.    I wrote Mr. Brown a summons for Petit larceny 18.2-96 and he signed it without issue. He was released from handcuffs and was told that he was free to go. PFC Sloane arrived back on scene and gave me particulars of the incident below;    Marshalls LPO said they saw a black male wearing all black enter the store at 1253 hours. When viewing the camera footage, the male is clearly Mr. Brown. Loss prevention also saw that an unknown female wearing a blue sweater entered the store at 1304 hours. Mr. Brown and the female were interacting with each other inside the store. Mr. Brown grabbed a bag from the front of the store and walked to the luggage aisle. He selected products and headed to the front of the store. At 1325 hours, Mr. Brown passed all points of sale and exited the store. He was stopped by Loss prevention and all products were returned. At 1329 hours the female suspect passed all points of sale with concealed merchandise and exited the store. She was stopped by Loss prevention and all of the products were recovered. I sent a link to loss prevention to submit the video to evidence.com.    Closed by arrest

Case Number: (739) 555-7210
Call For Service Number: E289659845

BWC active.   On May 16, 2025, at approximately 3:00pm, I was dispatched to a report of a larceny at 516 Hillcrest Rd. RP stated his vehicle was broken into and credit cards as well as an iPhone were stolen.   On scene, I made contact with Abdy outside the listed address in the parking lot. Due to a language barrier (Arabic), Language Line was used to better communicate. Abdy stated that he last saw his vehicle at 9:00am this morning. When he came out to the parking lot around 2:00pm, he noticed that three of his credit cards and an iPhone belonging to his friend (Mohamed) were gone. He stated that there was no physical damage to his vehicle. When he last left his vehicle in the parking lot this morning, he manually locked the driver side door after stepping of the vehicle.   Abdy is missing three total credit cards: Wells Fargo, CashApp, and Bank of America. He is not sure the end numbers on the cards. I advised him to cancel the cards as soon as he can. He stated he was on the phone with the banks now. Abdy stated his Bank of America card was used at several different locations in the Landmark Plaza. There were no timestamps to the transactions as they were still pending. Supplement will be added once timestamps are received from the victim and investigative measures are done with in store video footage.   In addition to the credit cards, Abdy`s friend, Mohamed, had his iPhone 15 Pro Max stolen. Case #2(671) 555-1101 will reflect this.   There were no surveillance cameras in the parking lot and the RP had no suspect details.   Inactive pending suspect information.

 BWC ACTIVATED    On 09/15/3955 Hillcrest Ave (ABC). The caller stated two subjects had left the store with merchandise without paying.    Arriving on scene I did not observe any persons in the shopping center parking lot matching the descriptions given. I then made contact with the staff who advised that at 2054 hours, two black males entered the location. The subjects took a bottle single bottle of tequila and then exited the store passing all points of sale.    I then observed camera footage and observed the two described males come into the store, browse for several minutes and then place a single bottle of Don Londres Tequila ($79.99) into a blue tote bag carried by one of the subjects. The subjects are identified as the following.    Subject 1  B/M, 5`9/5`10, 190lbs, white tee shirt, blue jean shorts, red sneakers, black backpack.    Subject 2  B/M, 5`8/5`9, 200lbs, white tee shirt, grey jeans, tan workstyle boots, blue tote bag on his left arm.    The subjects placed the bottle into the tote bag and then exited the store passing all points of sale at 2100 hours. The staff stated they did not recognize the two and they made no statements while inside the store besides asking staff if they were being followed. A link was provided for evidence.com to obtain the footage and the staff stated they would like to prosecute the subjects if found. I then provided a business card with the attached case number. No further actions taken by me at this time. Due to current investigative leads being exhausted, case held inactive pending further information.    INACTIVE

BWC Activated This event is related to 2(600) 555-2618  On 09/15/1986 I responded to 7940 Richmond Hwy (@CostCo) for a report of a larceny from a vehicle. When I arrived on scene I met with Jonathan Farmer (RP), who is an employee of CostCo.  Farmer advised that him and other employees observed a male subject enter two vehicles and stole items. Farmer record the subject inside of the vehicle and walking off the CostCo property with the stolen items in his hands. Employees followed the subject, which the subject dropped some of the items and continued to walk towards the Audubon Trailer park community. Farmer picked up the dropped items and brought them back to CostCo to return to the owner. Farm provided me pictures and videos of the subject, who was identified as Jose Romero Marquez (OF).  I then met with Tamarrius Whites (VI), who owned one of the vehicles Romero Marquez stole items from. Whites advised the CostCo employees returned $1,900 worth of items to him that he identified as his belongings.  Whites was given a business card with my name and the case number.  I then went to the Mount Vernon station and conducted a video magistrate call. I obtained a warrant on Romero Marquez for grand larceny and vehicle trespassing. The warrants were faxed to the front desk of the Mount Vernon station so the on duty PCA can fax the warrants to the warrant desk to be entered into 7412 Forest / 4347 OakSubpoena form was completed for Whites and Farmer.  All pictures and videos obtained related to this event are uploaded to evidence.com.  Case Inactive

incidents.narrative:
Case Number: (865) 555-5862
Call For Service Number: E290110639

 BWC ACTIVE  On May 15, 2025, at approximately 4:54 PM, I responded to a call regarding a suspected theft at the listed location. Upon arrival, I was briefed by the manger Timothey Weber, who had observed suspicious behavior from two individuals at the self-checkout area. The female suspect was described as African American, heavyset, wearing a black sleeveless shirt, blue pants, and a pink bonnet. The male suspect was described as African American, in his late forties or fifties, wearing a white, orange, and black t-shirt with orange shorts.   The manger reported that the female suspect appeared nervous and was frequently looking around while at the self-checkout. The manger was alerted by the cashier to monitor self-checkout number four. The manager observed that the suspects had rung up only five items despite having a cart full of merchandise. As the suspects were leaving, the female suspect placed a few items on the ledge, claiming she was not going to purchase them, and proceeded to leave with the rest of the items.   The officer approached the female suspect and asked for a receipt for the remaining items. She claimed to have split the payment with the male suspect, who had already left the store. The officer requested that she bring the male suspect back with the receipt. The male suspect returned but was unable to produce a receipt, claiming he might have thrown it away. The officer then pulled up the recent transactions from the self-checkout, none of which matched the items in question.  The male suspect continued to insist that the items were his and threatened to confront the female suspect. The manager suggested that the male suspect ask the female suspect to return and pay for the items. The male suspect did not respond, and the officer realized the female suspect might have left. The manager went outside and saw the female suspect in a red Jeep Patriot with 30-day tags B73418, preparing to leave.  The manager took a picture of the vehicle.   The female suspect confronted the officer, denying any theft and questioning why he was taking pictures of her car. She then admitted to having more items in the trunk and left the scene, yelling as she drove away. The manager noted the direction in which the vehicle left.   I was provided photos, and the manager will send security camera footage to evidence.com. Business card with case number was provided. Total amount taken was $100 worth of product. I ran the temp tag through 7066 Hillcrest / 9057 Elmand found it was registered to Courtney Mason. I ran her DMV photo with the photo that was provided to me by the manager, and they matched. Photo of the male suspect was sent out to other officers in attempts to identify.   Case held active pending warrants and identifying male suspect.     I acknowledge this report was generated using Draft One by Axon. I further acknowledge that I have reviewed the report in detail, made any necessary edits, and believe it to be an accurate representation of my recollection of the reported events. If needed, I am willing to testify to the accuracy of this report.

Case Number: (326) 555-4002
Call For Service Number: E245670696

 On May 15, 2025, I, Detective Bouey of the Auto Crimes Enforcement Unit (ACE), along with assisting ACE Detectives, responded to the Mount Vernon District in an attempt to locate what was ultimately identified as a 2021 black in color Chrysler minivan, which was displaying VA registration TKD2175.     VA Registration TKD2175 was listed in 7066 Hillcrest / 9057 Elmas a stolen license plate belonging to a 2009 white in color Saturn. Through my training, knowledge, and experience as an Auto Theft Detective, I know it is a common tactic for thieves to conduct a "tag-swap", where they steal license plates from one vehicle and replace them with other, usually stolen license plates, in an attempt to mask the theft of the "clean" license plates.    ACE Detectives located the black Chrysler, and followed the vehicle until it parked in the Walmart parking lot. ACE Detectives established a stakeout, and after a short time, two individuals, Mr. Travis Coleman (driver) and Ms. Nachelle Corbin (front seat passenger / daughter of registered owner) entered the vehicle. ACE Detectives converged on the vehicle and safely took all occupants into custody without issue.     It was quickly learned that both individuals will completely uninvolved and unknowing of the stolen license plates on their vehicle. Both occupants were released from handcuffs and were extremely polite, cooperative, and understanding of the situation.     ACE collected the stolen license plate (TKD2175) and packaged it to be returned to the DMV at the Mount Vernon District Station. ACE also entered the original correct license plate (TSU6663) as stolen. Only one license plate was stolen as the occupants were still in possession of the front license plate.     ACE handled all adminisitrative duties.     Case held as service.

A 4-day tow tag was issued on 12/10/1983 to a white 2012 Nissan Maxima (MD transporter tag# 5T11914) in relation to a parking complaint (PEO Greene`s complaint log# 2025-143). I conducted a search of the area and located the vehicle parked nearby on Providence Forest Dr. The vehicle was still in violation as it was displaying an invalid tag. The attached internal MVA record shows that Import Galleries which was issued the tag closed on 02/16/1993 and was invalidated at that time. The vehicle was then impounded and towed by Henry`s Wrecker Service (driver Rob) to 177 Fox St. Teletype employee 301682 entered the vehicle into the stored vehicle file (tty# E298876487). Case Number: (909) 555-1962
Call For Service Number: E242018136

On 10-25-1980, I responded to 5115 Leesburg Pike (Target) for a reported larceny in progress. The caller who was LPO on scene advised three subjects who were known to frequently steal items for the store were currently at the self checkout.    Just as I arrived on scene, I was met by the LPO who advised all subjects were in custody after being stopped in the front vestibule. It was further advised that the apprehended subject, Ms. Sameera Nazari, was accompanied by her two daughters, Nazila Nazari (12) and Madina Nazari (14) who she had used to select items from the store before proceeded to the self checkout kiosk. It was noted that once at the checkout counter Ms. Nazari and her daughter Madina would alternate scanning different items, while the other would place items directly from the shopping cart/basket into the shopping bags without scanning. Once all items from the shopping cart or basket were removed, all parties would continue past all points of sale with the unpaid items without rendering payment for the items. LPO`s were able to successfully apprehend Ms. Nazari and her children today without issue and they remained in their custody until PD arrived. All items were recovered which totaled ($513.56) for today`s incident. Furthermore, it was advised that this incident had been captured on in-store CCTV footage which showed Madina selecting items from around the store, filling up the shopping cart and then once at the self checkout counter, Madina and Ms. Nazari are seen moving unscanned items into bags before walking out of the store. All CCTV footage will be uploaded to evidence.com under the listed event number.    As further information was gathered, it was noted that Ms. Nazari and her children had been involved in seven previous larceny cases at the same location which had yet to be reported to PD. All incidents spanned from 09/01/1970 through today`s incident and involved the exact method of placing unscanned items from the shopping cart/basket into bags before passing all point of sale without rendering payment for the items. All incidents and totals are listed below and have been documented in a separate incident report:    09/01/1970 -Misc items held for sale ($384.42)  02/20/1998 -Misc items held for sale ($186.95)  11/26/1984 -Misc items held for sale (202.02)  09/15/1972 -Misc items held for sale (557.29)  01/14/1997 -Misc items held for sale ($155.79)  11/22/1979 -Misc items held for sale ($514.37)  02/13/1990 -Misc items held for sale ($367.16)    TOTAL FOR INCIDENTS: $2,881.56    Once all information was collected, it was determined that Ms. Nazari was believed to be using her young children to assist in the act of carrying out each larceny and was cited for eight counts of petit larceny (18.2-96) as well as eight counts of contributing to the delinquency of a minor (18.2-371). Once all summonses were executed Ms. Nazari was given all copies and advised she was free leave. Target LPO Andrew Moore did advise all parties they were banned from all Fairfax County Target stores for one year following today`s incident.    BWC ACTIVATED  Nothing Further

Case Number: (408) 555-7892
Call For Service Number: E207379509

 3797 River / 1062 WillowActivated***    On 04/24/1976 at around 21:1009 Meadow Dr. When I arrived on scene I observed the listed Dodge Van in the wooded area, with its rear and front lights on. I came into contact with Yuriy Lishchenko, who was in the driver seat with the keys in the ignition. While Yuriy was in the driver seat, in plain view there were clear baggies with white powdery substance in his right hand. Yuriy was not injured but was checked out by medics. He refused to go to the hospital.    Yuriy had a slurred speech, dilated pupils. Yuriy did not know where he was and did not know he drove off the road. He thought that he was in Herndon but we were in Centreville. After he was checked out from medics. He admitted to driving on Westfields Blvd. He was coming from a jobsite in Centreville. The time was 22:36, he said the accident was 20 minutes prior. Yuriy had no alcohol or drugs after the crash. Yuriy choice of drugs are marijuana and cocaine. The last time he said hes done cocaine was a week prior.  Yuriy refused to do SFST`s but blew into the PBT. He blew .000    I read him his Miranda rights as he agreed to speak with me. The white powdery substance that was in his hand he said was cocaine but there were not his. When I ask why they were in his hand he told me he thinks he was trying to move them.  He was then placed under arrest.  The VA tag on his van did not belong on the van at which it returned as sold.    He was transported to ADC with out incident. He was then brought to the ATU where he consented to have his blood drawn.  He was then brought in front of a magistrate were PC was presented. Warrants were issued and served for DWI 1st drugs, poss of SCH l/ll and Operate a Veh with out proper registration.  He was then booked, live scanned and transfer over to the custody of the Sheriff office.  His vehicle was towed by willow spring.    The drugs were packaged and sent out to be tested.    Closed by arrest.

incidents.narrative:
Case Number: (452) 555-6876
Call For Service Number: E249386046

On 10-25-1980, I responded to 5115 Leesburg Pike (Target) for a reported larceny in progress. The caller who was LPO on scene advised three subjects who were known to frequently steal items for the store were currently at the self checkout.    Just as I arrived on scene, I was met by the LPO who advised all subjects were in custody after being stopped in the front vestibule. It was further advised that the apprehended subject, Ms. Sameera Nazari, was accompanied by her two daughters, Nazila Nazari (12) and Madina Nazari (14) who she had used to select items from the store before proceeded to the self checkout kiosk. It was noted that once at the checkout counter Ms. Nazari and her daughter Madina would alternate scanning different items, while the other would place items directly from the shopping cart/basket into the shopping bags without scanning. Once all items from the shopping cart or basket were removed, all parties would continue past all points of sale with the unpaid items without rendering payment for the items. LPO`s were able to successfully apprehend Ms. Nazari and her children today without issue and they remained in their custody until PD arrived. All items were recovered which totaled ($513.56) for today`s incident. Furthermore, it was advised that this incident had been captured on in-store CCTV footage which showed Madina selecting items from around the store, filling up the shopping cart and then once at the self checkout counter, Madina and Ms. Nazari are seen moving unscanned items into bags before walking out of the store. All CCTV footage will be uploaded to evidence.com under the listed event number.    As further information was gathered, it was noted that Ms. Nazari and her children had been involved in seven previous larceny cases at the same location which had yet to be reported to PD. All incidents spanned from 09/01/1970 through today`s incident and involved the exact method of placing unscanned items from the shopping cart/basket into bags before passing all point of sale without rendering payment for the items. All incidents and totals are listed below and have been documented in a separate incident report:    09/01/1970 -Misc items held for sale ($384.42)  02/20/1998 -Misc items held for sale ($186.95)  11/26/1984 -Misc items held for sale (202.02)  09/15/1972 -Misc items held for sale (557.29)  01/14/1997 -Misc items held for sale ($155.79)  11/22/1979 -Misc items held for sale ($514.37)  02/13/1990 -Misc items held for sale ($367.16)    TOTAL FOR INCIDENTS: $2,881.56    Once all information was collected, it was determined that Ms. Nazari was believed to be using her young children to assist in the act of carrying out each larceny and was cited for eight counts of petit larceny (18.2-96) as well as eight counts of contributing to the delinquency of a minor (18.2-371). Once all summonses were executed Ms. Nazari was given all copies and advised she was free leave. Target LPO Andrew Moore did advise all parties they were banned from all Fairfax County Target stores for one year following today`s incident.    BWC ACTIVATED  Nothing Further

Case Number: (257) 555-7294
Call For Service Number: E242395564

***BWC and ICV Activated***  On April 16th, 2025 at approximately 1736 hours, I responded to the Lowes located at 6750 Richmond Hwy for a vehicle alert reference stolen license plate MD 9FM3758 displayed on a black Mercedes Benz SUV. The license plate returned to a White Honda Pilot.   Upon officers` arrival to the location, PFC Laitinen quickly observed the vehicle unoccupied, parked near the front of the Lowes, facing southbound. Due to the license plate being displayed on an entirely different make/model. I had a reasonable belief that the vehicle may be stolen as well. Due to this, officers formulated a plan to conduct a felony stop and prevent the vehicle from going mobile if a driver returned to it. PFC Laitinen restricted the channel and I observed his cruiser move forward, signalling that officers could move in. I activated my full emergency equipment (lights and siren) and positioned my vehicle at an angle facing the Mercedes` driver front bumper. I observed PFC Laitinen had his cruiser positioned directly in front of the Mercedes and was challenging the driver at gunpoint. I exited my cruiser after placing it into park and drew my department issued firearm, pointing it at the driver. I held the driver at gunpoint, voiced that I was doing so over the radio, and took over giving commands and challenging the driver after PFC Laitinen advised me to do so. The driver was given instructions to open his door, step out, and get into a kneeling position to be handcuffed. PFC Laitinen and PFC Ransom became the arrest team and handcuffed the driver, identified as Lamont Geiger.   Lamont was placed into the back seat of PFC Laitinen`s cruiser. Confirmation was started on the stolen license plate. PFC Ransom Mirandized Lamont; I asked Lamont several questions and he told me the following: Originally from California, Lamont purchased the Mercedes Benz almost one year ago (Vehicle showed a sell date in July in CA DMV). Lamont then moved to MD and brought his vehicle with him. Lamont stated that he had met a subject for the first time on a dating app and while spending time with him, becamae intoxicated. He then stated that the subject asked to driver Lamont`s vehicle and Lamont gave him permission to do. Lamont stated that he did not place the MD license plate onto the back of his vehicle. When officers removed the MD license plate the vehicle, it was discovered that the CA license plate actually belonging to Lamont`s vehicle was underneath the MD license plate; the MD license plate was placed on top of the CA license plate and held together with what appeared to be a string/rubber band. During the investigation, it was discovered that Lamont had several warrants to include one for stolen property involving a license plate on a rental vehicle. Lamont gave consent to search his vehicle; nothing of evidentiary value was located.  Lamont was released from handcuffs after I established local ties. Lamont was issued a summons for Possesion of Stolen Property and sent on his way.   The license plate was seized, will be packaged and turned into property to be sent back to the MD DMV.   Sgt. Russo and Sgt. Malloch were on scene and advised of the aforementioned UOF.  Nothing further to report. Case cleared by arrest.

 BWC ACTIVE***********      This report was generated using Draft One.    On April 6, 2025, at approximately 8:42 PM, I, Officer Ricketts responded to a call regarding a larceny at the Target store located at Springfield Mall Upon arrival, I positioned myself at the exit and entrance upstairs to monitor the situation.    I was informed by the Loss Prevention Officer (LPO) that there are 3 subjects in total. At the time of my arrival, I was informed that 2 of the subjects, a male and female, had exited the store with items and were outside in the parking lot while 1 female subject was still in the store now at self-check out. The female subject still in the store, identified by her pink hat, had attempted to leave the store without paying for several items. The subject was accompanied by a child, presumed to be her son. The LPO indicated that the suspect had initially concealed items in her purse and attempted to pass all points of sale before being stopped and subsequently returning to pay for the items.    While monitoring the situation, I communicated with other officers to ensure coverage of the shopping center and to locate any additional subjects.    The LPO and I approached the suspect, who was heading toward the exit. I confirmed with the LPO that she had indeed passed all points of sale, with items still not paid for, constituting an larceny. The suspect, who did not speak English, was being assisted by her son for translation. To ensure clear communication, I utilized the language line service to convey the situation to the subject. At the beginning of our interaction the subject did rush into the women`s bathroom and placed unpaid merchandise that was in concealed in her pink purse, in the trash. The subject, later identified as Ms. Doris, was seen on camera concealing items in her purse and was seen on camera not paying for those items at self-check out.    The LPO provided video evidence showing the suspect and two other individuals concealing items and attempting to leave the store without payment. Ms. Doris panicked and returned to pay for the items she had exposed, while the other two individuals left the store with unpaid items. All actions were recorded on video.    I detained Ms. Doris and coordinated with other officers to locate the additional suspects. The suspect`s son confirmed that they had arrived with two other individuals in a black Honda Civic. Ms. Doris did show me the key to that vehicle and then later recanted and stated that she had arrived by bus.    During the investigation, the additional concealed items were found in the bathroom trash can. These items were collected as evidence. The suspect continued to deny any wrongdoing, claiming she was only asking for the bathroom location when first stopped by the LPO. Despite her claims, the video evidence clearly showed her passing all points of sale with concealed items.    I ask Ms. Doris for her name, address, date of birth and social security number and or drivers license several times. She refused to provide any of the information. After several attempts to obtain her information, the suspect finally provided her name, address, and social security number through the language line interpreter.    Officer Miller and Officer Moser were able to locate the outstanding subjects who did have unpaid items with them. The 2 outstanding subjects were identified as Mr. Kevin and Ms. Rosa. All 3 subjects were detained and remained in the LPO office.    The LPO confirmed that Ms. Doris had paid for some items but had concealed others, which were not paid for, and Mr. Kevin and Ms. Rosa did not pay for any of the times. Mr. Kevin did break a vase while leaving the store with the LPO allowed him to pay for which he did. The total value of the unpaid items was calculated, and all 3341 Hillcrest Way on the listed date and time for the offense. They were both released from the store. A ban letter f...

*BWC ACTIVE*  On March 28, 2025, at approximately 14:44 hours, I responded to the Walgreens located at 1943 Oak Ln. for a report of a larceny that had just occurred.  Upon arrival, I met with the store manager Gilma Sanchez, who stated that a Hispanic male, wearing all black, carrying a blue backpack, entered the store, grabbed a case of Modelo beers, and walked out, bypassing all points of sale without payment. Gilma was able to provide a picture of the suspect that she took on her personal phone.  Based on the image, I was able to quickly identify the suspect as Tereso Ramos, who is known to be a transient in the Mason District area. Gilma was provided with a business card and notified of the case status.  Prior to clearing, I checked known hangout spots for Tereso. I was able to locate him behind 3492 Pine Rd wearing the same clothes and sitting next to the case of beers that he had stole. Tereso was detained and issued a summons for petit larceny. The stolen item was recovered and returned to Walgreens.  ARREST

Case Number: (903) 555-5924
Call For Service Number: E214500156

On 08-23-1970 I was actively looking for Devin Warren who shoplifted from Target (6311 River Dr) in case (641) 555-1080. Devin was identified by other Mason officers after reviewing camera footage. I was informed that Devin was last seen in the parking lot of the McDonalds across the street (8323 Cedar Way) Devin was said to be wearing the same clothing as in the larceny and was possibly in a black Toyota Corolla with Virginia tag GAMMA16.  It was also discovered that Devin was wanted out of Newport News Virginia for failing to appear.  I entered the parking lot of the McDonalds around 2308 hours and observed the black Toyota Corolla with matching tag. I observed Devin sitting in the driver seat. two other females, Yemi Yoc Riquiac and Jaylee Matute were identified as passengers. I continued driving past, advised of the situation via radio, and waited for backup. 401A (PFC Lacey) arrived on scene and made contact with Devin at the driver side if the car. PFC Lacey informed Devin that he was being detained and explained that it was related to the warrant and larceny. During this, PFC Lacey observed a glass pipe, containing black residue, laying on Devin`s lap. PFC Lacey, along with grabbing Devin`s arm, gave Devin commands which Devin did not obey. Devin immediately pulled away and climbed over the center console of the car. Devin pushed he was toward the back seat, and made his way to the passenger seat. During this, he was pulling away from PFC Lacey.  I went to the passenger side of the vehicle were I grabbed Devin`s right wrist in an attempt to control his arm and movement. The goal was to place Devin in handcuffs. Devin continued to pull away and resist. Devin was given numerous commands that he was detained, not free to leave, and was told to stop resisting. Devin exited the car and I pushed him into the vehicle door jamb in an attempt limit his movement. 410A (PFC Villalobos) quickly arrived and began to assist. At this time PFC Lacey, PFC Villalobos and myself continued this struggle attempting to place Devin in handcuffs. Devin continued to resist and push through officers as he tried to flee. Devin began to climb onto the trunk of the car. PFC Lacey and I grabbed Devin`s legs and pulled him off the car. Devin did not go completely to the ground. I then reached from behind and grabbed Devin in a bear hug around his waist which was very effective in controlling his movements. Officers were then able to slowly work Devin`s arm behind his back and place him in handcuffs. Devin sustained several small scratched on the front and rear of his torso. These scratches are likely from equipment on officers vest. Rescue was refused and supervisor updated on scene.  After the struggle, Devin turned to me and said "I had to make ya`ll work for it, buddy".  The pipe was recovered. Devin also had a ball of foil in his pocket that contained black residue. Devin`s handcuffs were adjusted and checked for fit. Devin was placed in my cruiser which was searched prior to and after transport. Devin unbuckled his seatbelt and I had to buckle it again. Under Miranda, Devin did admit to being in possession of the stolen merchandise from Target however, it was not located. Miscellaneous drug paraphernalia was located in the vehicle (straws, foil, plastic bag) which will be packaged for destruction and placed in the locker at the Mason Station. The pipe from Devin`s lap will also be packaged and placed in the locker at the Mason Station. RFLE completed.   Newport News warrant confirmed and faxed to the ADC. I transported Devin to the ADC without incident. At the ADC, Devin refused to sit down in the sally port while I checked in. Devin was argumentative and I notified deputies that he was combative.  I went before magistrate Hong were I swore out and served a warrant for possession of 5904 Meadow / 5761 Lake, obstruction of justice, petit larceny, and served the Newport News warran...

Case Number: (409) 555-3188
Call For Service Number: E209131303

 BWC ACTIVE  On 07/01/1975, I responded to 6166 Leesburg Pike (Jefferson Apts) for a narcotics event. Before my arrival I spoke with the complainant who advised an involved party(male) was leaving the scene wearing a Brown Jacket.  I saw the male wearing the brown jacket, I made contact with the gentleman and immediately I recognized his face as a wanted subject, identified as Quynh Tran. I asked the subject for his name and date of birth and he replied with Giang Tran. I asked for his date of birth, the subject replied with 04-08-1971 and then said 1974. I knew the subject was lying. I then advised him to stop lying to me. Mr. Tran then gave me his real information being Quynh Tran 11-06-1972.  After confirming his Identification I conducted a persons check with the assistance of DPSC, where they advised Mr. Tran was wanted from Fairfax County and other Jurisdictions. I detained Mr. Tran whilst DPSC was confirming his warrants. Once Mr. Tran`s warrants were confirmed  I placed him under arrest. I conducted a search incident to arrest of Mr. Tran, I located on his left pocket 3 little white rocks, based on my training and experience I know this to be crack cocaine. I located a concealed spring stick baton on his right pocket side.  I placed Mr. Tran on the right rear seat of my cruiser which was inspected before and after transporting him to the ADC. Once at the ADC, I served one 18.2-96 Warrant from Herndon PD, I served two 18.2-96 from Loudon County. I also served a grand larceny warrant out of Fairfax County. I also sought additional charges today. I testified to a 18.2-250 warrant and a 18.2-308.2 warrant. Magistrate Wiggs found enough probable cause to issue me the charges.  I called Loudon County and Ein 3765 advised she received the fax of the warrants. I then called Herndon`s dispatch center and Ein 5230 advised they received the warrants. I then called the warrant desk and EIN 304490 advised she will remove Mr. Tran from the system.   The suspcted Crack cocaine will be sent to the lab for testing. The string stick baton will be in the Mason Station Locker room as evidence.   Case Closed arrest.

Call For Service Number: E281351624

***BWC AND ICV ACTIVATED***  On 07/08/1981, I was in the area of 9811 Lake Pl, I then turned around and conducted a traffic stop.  I informed the driver of the vehicle that I was unable to get a reading from the rear license plate. I asked the driver who was the owner of the vehicle. The passenger then stated that the owner of the vehicle was her sister. The passenger then stated she was unable to get in contact with her sister and she could not provide me with any proof of registration. The driver, Fidelina Zetino, provided me with her drivers license and I copied the VIN from the vehicle`s dash board. After running the driver through 2011 Cedar / 3572 Springdiscovered she had a outstanding warrant. The vehicle returned unregistered registered when running the VIN through 9326 Willow / 1721 JuniperAfter confirming the warrant with the warrant desk, I placed Fidelina under arrest.  I requested Red Mans towing company to impound the vehicle as there was no proof of a registered owner of the vehicle. Officer on scene conducted a vehicle inventory of the vehicle and found a glass pipe with burnt residue on the driver seat. Due to my training and experience I identified the item as a crack pipe.  I transported Fidelina to the ADC and obtained charges from a magistrate for possession of a controlled substance 1 or 2. The magistrate also issued the charges for the warrant from PWC and set $1,000 secured bond. Fidelina was then TOT FCSO.  The glass pipe with burnt residue was packaged and turned into property with a completed RFLE. The substance will be sent to the lab for analysis. I faxed over a served copy of Fidelina`s warrants to PWC.  Case status closed by arrest

The complaining party advised dispatch a drunk customer who had just waked out on a tab at Teds Bulletin restaurant. RP informed dispatch the offender who was later identified as Evan Harrison-Bibb (OF) was refusing to pay. That incident was documented under case 2(452) 555-7057. Case Number: (505) 555-7967
Call For Service Number: E269554652

 TUT Uniform  On 12/17/1986 Elite Jewelers contacted the Tysons Urban Team in reference to a larceny that had just occurred.  Upon my arrival at the location I spoke with Ms. Habib, the manager of the location, and Ms. Bouchouary, the employee who assisted the subject. They advised that a B/M subject wearing a blue Burberry coat, khaki pants, and a checkered hat had entered the location twice. They stated that upon his first entry the subject looked around and asked about the Rolex watches. He then told them that he was going to check a different store and may return. During the first interaction with Elite Jewelers staff the subject was accompanied by another B/M wearing a black jacket with white/black trim, a gray undershirt, black pants and tennis shoes. A short time later the subject returned alone and asked to try on a Submariner Rolex valued at $19,000. Ms. Bouchouary requested the subject`s identification as collateral per Elite Jeweler`s policy. The subject provided a TX ID bearing the name Dayan, Steven.  Ms. Bouchouary provided the subject with the requested watch which he then applied to his wrist. The subject asked where the mirror was so that he could see how it looked. Ms. Bouchouary indicated the mirror in the rear of the store and began walking with the subject towards the mirror. They had only walked a short distance when the subject spun on his heel and ran out of the door. Ms. Habib attempted to follow the subject however by the time she exited the store she could could no longer see him.  A check of mall security cameras revealed that the subject did not go to any other jeweler but instead stood in the area of Macy`s circle observing the Elite Jewelers. Eventually the subject entered the Elite Jewelers and upon exiting can be seen turning into one of the maintenance hallways directly beside Elite Jewelers. The subject is then observed getting into a gray Volvo bearing VA tag OMEGA16. This tag returned to a Hyundai registered to Mr. William Atwood. Upon contacting Mr. Atwood he advised that his son, Mr. Aadon Atwood had driven their Hyundai to Patsy`s American Grill located at 1194 Sycamore St the car until replacement plates could be obtained from the DMV.  I spoke with Mr. Emanuel Bunch the manager of the security service who`s area of responsibility included the parking lot where Mr. Atwood had parked his vehicle. Mr. Bunch was able to provide video of the gray Volvo pulling into the location bearing NY tags LJZ8238. The vehicle parked and a subject can be seen exiting the vehicle and ducking down at the front of another vehicle parked nose in. After several minutes the subject got back into the Volvo then the Volvo exited the location. The Volvo bearing LJZ8238 is then found to be on Tysons Corner Center property at 1402 hrs. Upon checking the through law enforcement resources the vehicle was found to be a Hertz rental.  Hertz rental was contacted and advised that they would have a representative contact me however as of the writing of this report they have not done so. At this time no suspect information is available. Case will be updated as investigation continues.  No further action taken

Case Number: (544) 555-4540
Call For Service Number: E226992759

On 06/26/7693 Lake Ave Virginia ABC store. When I arrived onscene I observed that people were leaving the store and noticed that no was in immediate danger. As I approached the business the store manager, Michelle Duncan, met me outside to advise me that the everyone in the store was fine and that two B/M`s stole multiple bottles of alcohol. She advised me that a 30-40 year old B/M wearing a green beanie, black ski mask, tinted sunglasses, black puffy coat, black pants, and brown shoes. He entered the store first. He was also holding a colorful reusable grocery bag. Then a 50-60 year old B/M wearing a black baseball hat, brown jacket, black pants, brown shoes, had a COVID mask on, and was holding a blind cane entered the store. He was also holding a recycleable clear grocery bag. She advised me that she thought that he was blind due to his cane and that she opened the door for him. The two suspects then stole:  Patron Extra Anejo ($94.99) x4  Don Julio Reposado Primavera Tequila ($79.99) x1  Don Julio Tequila Anejo ($71.99) X2  Don Julio Tequila Reposado ($66.99) X2  1800 Tequila Reposado ($47.99) X1  Nikolai Vodka ($7.49) x1  The younger B/M stole $705.91 and the older B/M stole $87.48.    Mrs. Duncan then advised that the she attempted to assist the younger B/M suspect because she could tell what was going on. She then grabbed the colorful shopping bag that the suspect has on the ground and the suspect uses one arm to lightly shove her off of the bag. He then left the store. She then pushed the holdup alarm button. The older B/M walked into the store and stole two alcohol bottles and walked out of the store after the younger B/M. The suspects then got into separate vehicles and drove away. The younger B/M was driving an older black Hyundai Elantra and the older B/M was driving a Black Chevy SUV.    After speaking to Mrs. Duncan. I spoke to a witness, Douglas Dunn. He advised that he took a picture of the license plate of the Black Chevy SUV, TRJ3777//VA. Case Number: (888) 555-5368
Call For Service Number: E209747335

On 10/05/1979, at approximately 1550hrs, I was dispatched to 1979 Lake Pl. at the Giant for a larceny report that occurred on September 30th 2024.    When I arrived on scene, I spoke with the loss prevention officer , Mr. Payne who advised me that on the date listed above, at approximately 1611hrs, three hispanic males entered the store and loaded a kart full of tide detergent. All three males then took the kart full of tide detergent passed all points of sale without paying and left the store. Mr. Payne was able to total the amount of Tide detergent to $347.88. When asked if he wanted to prosecute if the suspects could be located he said yes and was able to provide me video evidence of this incident.    While reviewing the video footage, I was able to observe that the three males involved were all hispanic males and looked to me in the early 20`s. One male had longer hair with a beanie style hate, the other male had medium hair and tattoos and the other male had shorter hair and a smaller statue build.      REF case 2(254) 555-2314 and case 2(595) 555-2957// All three subjects entered the store shortly after I cleared from scene and committed a separate larceny on today`s date at 1636hrs. All three suspects were identified on scene as the same suspects in the video footage Mr. Payne provided.    Subject 1, Mr. Garcia Ayala 20 years of age, was charged with misdemeanor larceny for this incident. Subject 2, Mr. Sanchez Garcia 18 years of age, was charged with misdemeanor larceny for this incident as well. The third subject was a juvenile and was released to the care and custody of his father.    I brought Mr. Garcia Ayala and Mr. Sanchez Garcia to the adult detention center where multiple larceny warrants could be served. I brought both suspects in front of magistrate Michaele Crosby who found probable cause for these particular charges of misdemeanor petit larceny. Both Mr. Garcia Ayala and Mr. Sanchez Garcia were photographed and finger printed at the ADC and were released on a $2,500.1703 Elm Pl house. No further action taken.    My Axon body camera was utilized. This case is closed by arrest.

Peal and personally conducted interviews of the complainant/victim.

Case Number: (901) 555-3344
Call For Service Number: E202894089

 --==8382 Willow / 7236 Hawthorn==--    On Saturday December 16, 2023, members of the FCPD 2nd District responded to the listed location in reference to a domestic dispute in progress called in by residents of the complex. Members had earlier been out to the listed location (E254607153) and attempted to speak with parties on scene who refused to answer the door. Upon members arriving back on scene, the undersigned (Pfc. R Devanney # 358513) and 221B (Pfc. M. Romero # 352071) knocked and announced at the front door identifying as police. Members could hear a male and female arguing inside the location in addition to sounds of a possible assault in progress. Members continued to knock and announce until the male subject herein known as OF-2 (TAYLOR, KEITH ASHLEY II) opened the door with the female subject herein known as OF-1 (FLOYD, PRINCESS) directly behind him.    Members noted that both OF-1 and OF-2 appeared to be under the influence of a controlled substance with both sweating profusely with dilated pupils. OF-1 then attempted to slam the door shut on 221B as the undersigned noted that OF-1 was holding a lighter with a straw containing residue in her hand. Based upon officers prior training and experience the listed straw is consistent with the use and consumption of counterfeit pills known to contain Fentanyl. Members then advised OF-1 that she was being detained which resulted in OF-1 pulling away and resisting officers commands. OF-1 was observed to be wearing only a longsleeve shirt with no pants on before OF-1 yelled that she had to pee as officers were attempting to secure her in handcuffs. OF-1 then began to urinate profusely before she began to start flailing her body around in order to deliberately urinate on the undersigned (striking right upper leg) and 221B (striking lower body and legs). Officers were then able to secure OF-1 in handcuffs before she was quickly able to break one hand free out of the handcuff and resist officers. OF-1 was then taken to the ground and secured in handcuffs before she requested officers bring her back inside the residence.    Upon entering the residence members observed OF-2 who was now in the rear of the location towards the family room window. Members then observed a plastic bag on the floor containing multiple rounds on 9mm ammunition in addition to a digital scale on top of a end table with a white powder on-top of it which based upon officers training and experience is consistent with MDMA commonly referred to as "Molly". Members additionally observed burnt foil with residue on a bookshelf in addition burnt foil on-top of the bedroom bed after OF-1 requested officers to retrieve clothing for her from the room. Based upon the ammunition and narcotics in plainview officers detained OF-2 as an investigation was conducted.    Both OF-1 and OF-2 denied that an altercation had occurred and stated that they were only having a verbal argument. Cruiser 020B (Sgt. Roberts) was advised and notification were made to FCPD OCN who advised that narcotics detectives would be responding to the scene.    Upon members of OCN arriving on scene OF-1 was read her Miranda warning and admitted that the substance on-top the scale was "Molly". OF-1 additionally signed a consent form authorizing members to search the residence. A search of the residence yielded no additional concealed narcotics or weapons. OF-1 further stated that she and OF-2 had been using Molly earlier.    During the listed search members observed OF-2 become extremely nervous as he kept repeating that he wanted to go. Members advised OF-2 that he was detained and was not free to go multiple times as he appeared to be experiencing a possible panic attack. FCFD subsequently responded to the scene to evaluate OF-2 who refused to allow medics to examine him.    Following the investigation OF-1 and OF-2 were placed under arrest for Possession of a Controlled Substance (18...
