Case Number: (973) 555-8495
Call For Service Number: E299080827

 --==8382 Willow / 7236 Hawthorn==--    On Saturday November 4, 2023, members of the FCPD 2nd District responded to the listed location for a warrant service for IO-1 (RUNALDUE, JEREMY MICHAEL) in reference to FCPD case 2(336) 555-4742. Upon arrival on scene members knocked and announced at the front door after hearing the voices of multiple people inside the residence. The listed location is known to members to be an active location used for narcotics sales and use within the district. After announcing, members heard the listed voices go silent as members continued to knock before being met at the front door by IO-2 (RUNALDUE, EVELYN PIERPOINT) who is IO-1s mother.    Upon speaking to IO-2 she stated that IO-1 was not currently at home and initally stated no one else was home before members advised her they cleared heard other voices. Members then observed multiple other people being to move around in the residence when members observed OF-1 (BREWSTER, FLORISHA LYNN) inside the location who was known to members to have active warrants for her arrest held by Fairfax County.    Units then entered the location and took OF-1 into custody in reference to the listed warrants without incident and escorted her outside of the residence. While inside the location members observed paraphernalia such as burnt foil and unknown pills consistent with the illegal use of controlled substances.    After escorting OF-1 outside the residence members observed IO-1 ride towards the location on a bike before stopping and fleeing the area on foot after seeing officers at his residence. A check of the surrounding area for IO-1 by members met with negative results.    9530 Sycamore / 9741 Oakcheck of OF-1 returned with active warrants for her arrest held by Fairfax County and Prince William County Police Departments. During a search incident to arrest of OF-1s purse the undersigned located a multi color capsule which was found to contain white powdery residue which based upon the undersigneds training and experience is consistent with Methylenedioxymethamphetamine (MDMA). OF-1 was read her Miranda warning by 221B (Ofc. Laing) and agreed with answer questions. When asked about the listed powder OF-1 stated that it was "Molly" which is a common street term to refer to MDMA. A Tylenol pill bottle was also located in the listed purse which was found to contain unknown pills that OF-1 stated were "antibiotics". A straw and knife with burnt residue were also recovered from the listed purse. OF-1 was then transported to the ADC for further processing without incident.    Upon arrival at the ADC, OF-1 was brought before a on-duty Magistrate where the listed warrants were executed. Further testimony was given and a warrant for Possession of a Schedule I/II Controlled Substance (18.2-250) was obtained and executed. OF-1 was then denied bond, livescanned and was turned over to the FCSO for further processing without incident.    ITEMS: 1594430, 1594431, and 1594432 were recovered as Evidence and secured in Mount Vernon District Station Drug Locker pending transport to DFS for further testing. ITEM: 1594433 was secured in the district property locker marked for Destruction.    Warrant Desk EIN: 307185 was notified and cleared the listed Fairfax County Warrants from 3183 Juniper / 780 Oakserved copy of the Prince William County warrant for OF-1 was faxed to the Prince William County Warrant Unit ((859) 555-4530) in addition to a Teletype message regarding OF-1s bond status.    Case closed by arrest.

 ***BWC active***  On 01/09/1984, I was looking up active warrants in my patrol area when I noticed that Ricky Brooks was showing as having two active warrants in iLeads. I confirmed that he was also showing a wanted hit in 6513 Forest / 2546 Juniperam familiar with Mr. Brooks and have previously encountered him during investigative detentions. I am aware that he will state that his name is "Antonio" when asked to identify himself, which is actually his middle name.  I began searching the area of Patrick Henry Dr, which I know Mr. Brooks to frequent. I quickly located him in the parking lot of the McDonald`s at 8323 Cedar Way. Shortly after seeing my cruiser turn around to make contact with him, Mr. Brooks and the subject he was with began to walk away from where they were standing. I exited my cruiser, and verbally addressed Mr. Brooks as "Ricky" to which he stated that he was "Antonio". I advised Mr. Brooks he was detained while I investigated the outstanding warrants and placed him in handcuffs without incident.  Mr. Brooks maintained that his first name was Antonio. I asked him to confirm his birthday, which he stated was 07/14/1987 -- one day off from his actual birthday.  The two outstanding warrants were confirmed in hand at the warrant desk and I advised Mr. Brooks that he was now under arrest. Prior to my search, Mr. Brooks stated that he had "meth" on him. I located several small, white, crystal rocks wrapped in tin foil from the center front pocket of his sweatshirt. I mirandized Mr. Brooks and he again stated that the substance was "meth". Mr. Brooks declined to speak with a detective.  I transported Mr. Brooks to the ADC with my cruiser`s ICV activated without incident while stopping at the warrant desk to retrieve the outstanding warrants. I brought Mr. Brooks before the magistrate and executed the warrants. Additionally, Magistrate Schwennesen issued a new warrant for one count of Possession of a Schedule II Substance, which I executed in his presence. Mr. Brooks wanted hits in 7066 Hillcrest / 9057 Elmand iLeads were removed by Warrant Desk Employee #304490.  Mr. Brooks was photographed, live scanned and turned over to the custody of the deputies. He was held on no bond.  The suspected methamphetamine was seized, packaged and submitted for laboratory testing at the Mason Station Property Room.  No further actions. Closed Arrest. Info Copy - Gangs

Case Number: (698) 555-8449
Call For Service Number: E235209996

 I responded to the listed address for the report of a larceny. Once on scene, I spoke with the RP (Mr. Stiiff) who advised that two individuals had past point of sale with items which total value of $36.00. The individuals would give back the items, but refused to stay on scene. Their description/direction of travel was given, and 221A (OFC Wilkerson) would search the area for the offenders.  221A would notify me that he was out with two suspects matching the description. I obtained photos of the offenders and responded to the scene. 221A had detained the offenders identified as OF1 (Mr. Mcknight) and OF2 (Ms. Ward). OF1 would admit to attempting to steal the items. The RP would advise me that he would like to press charges, and so a I wrote OF1 a summons for petit larceny which he signed.  OF2 had a warrant out for her arrest and the warrant was confirmed. Due to a cast she was wearing (broken arm), I did not restrain her in handcuffs as she was cooperative. I transported her to the warrant desk to pick up the warrant, and then the ADC. At the ADC, she was served with the warrant and given $1000 unsecured bond. She was then fingerprinted and subsequently released. The warrant was for a violation of supervised release. The warrant was taken out of the system.  Nothing further. Case close by arrest.

incidents.narrative:
Case Number: (676) 555-9979
Call For Service Number: E229070971

On 04/19/170 Maple St/Richmond Hwy going southbound for an assault in progress. The caller advised a handicap individual was getting beat on.    Upon arrival, I observed multiple subjects at the bus stop and observed a black man wearing all black walk across the street to the northbound Lukens Ln/Richmond Hwy bus stop. I observed Aziekweh Lewis Baldwin sitting down on the bench with bloody knees. Aziekweh refused medical attention and immediately stated he did not want to press charges or speak to me but said to speak to the man across the street at the bus stop what had happened.     Aziekweh eventually explained that the subject had struck his head and mushed his head against the glass of the bus stop and that he had knocked his contacts out of his eyes. He stated that he believed his necklace had fallen off at some point and believed the subject had also taken his necklace when he saw it on the floor.     The suspect was identified as Nigel McDonald, stated that nothing had occurred and that Aziekweh is his brother. Nigel then changed his story and stated that he had pushed Aziekweh after he had punched him in the face. He did not have visible marks on his face. He also admitted to having the necklace and stated he would give his brother his necklace back after he had calmed down.    Aziekweh was provided a business card with the case number and explained how he could obtain his own warrants for the subject. Aziekweh`s friend arrived to take him back to their place for the night. Aziekweh was giving his necklace back. He stated he would be getting his own warrants.    BWC was activated.    Nothing further.
